passage_title: Do literate women make better mothers?

sentences.sentence: Children in developing countries are healthier and more likely to survive past the age of five when their mothers can read and write.
sentences.translation: 在发展中国家，当母亲会读写时，孩子更健康，也更可能活过五岁。

structure.text: are healthier and more likely to survive past the age of five
when their mothers can read and write

difficult_points: when 从句说明母亲具备读写能力这一条件。
be more likely to 表示更可能，survive past 指活过某个年龄。

sentences.sentence: Experts in public health accepted this idea decades ago, but until now no one has been able to show that a woman's ability to read in itself improves her children's chances of survival.
sentences.translation: 公共卫生专家几十年前就接受了这一观点，但直到现在，还没有人能证明女性的阅读能力本身会提高其子女的生存机会。

structure.text: but until now no one has been able to show
that a woman's ability to read in itself improves her children's chances of survival

difficult_points: but 后用现在完成时 has been able，强调直到现在仍未被证实。
in itself 强调阅读能力本身，排除其他伴随因素。

sentences.sentence: Most literate women learnt to read in primary school, and the fact that a woman has had an education may simply indicate her family's wealth or that it values its children more highly.
sentences.translation: 大多数识字女性是在小学学会阅读的，而女性受过教育这一事实，可能只是表明她的家庭较富裕，或更重视孩子。

structure.text: learnt to read in primary school
and the fact that a woman has had an education
may simply indicate her family's wealth or that it values its children more highly

difficult_points: that a woman has had an education 修饰 fact，说明事实内容。
后一个 that 引导 indicate 的并列宾语，it 指 her family。

sentences.sentence: Now a long-term study carried out in Nicaragua has eliminated these factors by showing that teaching reading to poor adult women, who would otherwise have remained illiterate, has a direct effect on their children's health and survival.
sentences.translation: 如今，一项在尼加拉瓜开展的长期研究通过证明：教贫困成年女性阅读会直接影响其子女的健康和生存，从而排除了这些因素；若非如此，她们本会一直不识字。

structure.text: a long-term study carried out in Nicaragua
by showing that teaching reading to poor adult women
who would otherwise have remained illiterate
has a direct effect on their children's health and survival

difficult_points: carried out in Nicaragua 是过去分词，修饰 study。
would otherwise have remained 表示与事实相反的过去假设，who 指 women。

sentences.sentence: In 1979, the government of Nicaragua established a number of social programmes, including a National Literacy Crusade.
sentences.translation: 1979年，尼加拉瓜政府设立了多项社会项目，其中包括全国扫盲运动。
difficult_points: including... 补充举例，修饰 social programmes。
a number of 后接复数名词，表示若干。

sentences.sentence: By 1985, about 300,000 illiterate adults from all over the country, many of whom had never attended primary school, had learnt how to read, write and use numbers.
sentences.translation: 到1985年，全国约30万名不识字成年人学会了读、写和使用数字，其中许多人从未上过小学。

structure.text: about 300,000 illiterate adults from all over the country
many of whom had never attended primary school
had learnt how to read, write and use numbers

difficult_points: By 1985 与过去完成时 had learnt 搭配，说明在该时间点前完成。
many of whom 指这些成年人中的许多人。

sentences.sentence: During this period, researchers from the Liverpool School of Tropical Medicine, the Central American Institute of Health in Nicaragua, the National Autonomous University of Nicaragua and the Costa Rican Institute of Health interviewed nearly 3,000 women, some of whom had learnt to read as children, some during the literacy crusade and some who had never learnt at all.
sentences.translation: 在此期间，来自多个机构的研究人员访谈了近3000名女性：有些在儿童时期学会阅读，有些在扫盲运动期间学会，还有些完全没有学过。

structure.text: researchers from the Liverpool School of Tropical Medicine, the Central American Institute of Health in Nicaragua, the National Autonomous University of Nicaragua and the Costa Rican Institute of Health
some of whom had learnt to read as children, some during the literacy crusade and some who had never learnt at all

difficult_points: 极长主语的核心是 researchers，from 后列出其机构来源。
some of whom、some、some who 三组并列，按学会阅读的经历分类。

sentences.sentence: The women were asked how many children they had given birth to and how many of them had died in infancy.
sentences.translation: 研究人员询问这些女性生了多少孩子，以及其中有多少在婴儿期死亡。

structure.text: how many children they had given birth to
and how many of them had died in infancy

difficult_points: were asked 是被动语态，两个 how many 从句均为询问内容。
them 指 children；give birth to 是“生下”的固定搭配。

sentences.sentence: The research teams also examined the surviving children to find out how well-nourished they were.
sentences.translation: 研究团队还检查了存活儿童的营养状况。

structure.text: to find out how well-nourished they were

difficult_points: surviving 修饰 children，指存活下来的儿童。
to find out 表示检查目的，how well-nourished 引导间接疑问从句。

sentences.sentence: The investigators' findings were striking.
sentences.translation: 调查人员的发现十分引人注目。
difficult_points: investigators' 是所有格，表示调查人员的。
striking 此处表示显著、令人印象深刻。

sentences.sentence: In the late 1970s, the infant mortality rate for the children of illiterate mothers was around 110 deaths per thousand live births.
sentences.translation: 20世纪70年代末，不识字母亲所生儿童的婴儿死亡率约为每千例活产110人死亡。

structure.text: the infant mortality rate for the children of illiterate mothers
around 110 deaths per thousand live births

difficult_points: for the children of... 限定死亡率所针对的群体。
per thousand live births 是统计口径，表示每千例活产。

sentences.sentence: At this point in their lives, those mothers who later went on to learn to read had a similar level of child mortality (105/1000).
sentences.translation: 在她们人生的这一阶段，那些后来继续学习阅读的母亲，其子女死亡率也相近（每千例105人）。

structure.text: At this point in their lives
who later went on to learn to read
a similar level of child mortality

difficult_points: those mothers 指后来参加识字教育的母亲，who 从句限制对象。
went on to learn 表示随后继续去学习。

sentences.sentence: For women educated in primary school, however, the infant mortality rate was significantly lower, at 80 per thousand.
sentences.translation: 不过，对在小学受过教育的女性而言，婴儿死亡率显著更低，为每千例80人。

structure.text: For women educated in primary school

difficult_points: educated... 修饰 women，是过去分词后置定语。
at 80 per thousand 补充具体统计数值。

sentences.sentence: In 1985, after the National Literacy Crusade had ended, the infant mortality figures for those who remained illiterate and for those educated in primary school remained more or less unchanged.
sentences.translation: 1985年，在全国扫盲运动结束后，仍不识字者与小学受教育者的婴儿死亡率数据都大致没有变化。

structure.text: after the National Literacy Crusade had ended
the infant mortality figures for those who remained illiterate and for those educated in primary school

difficult_points: after 从句用过去完成时，表示扫盲运动先结束。
those who... 与 those educated... 是两个并列比较群体。

sentences.sentence: For those women who learnt to read through the campaign, the infant mortality rate was 84 per thousand, an impressive 21 points lower than for those women who were still illiterate.
sentences.translation: 对通过这场运动学会阅读的女性来说，婴儿死亡率为每千例84人，比仍不识字的女性低21点，降幅令人印象深刻。

structure.text: For those women who learnt to read through the campaign
an impressive 21 points lower than for those women who were still illiterate

difficult_points: who learnt... 修饰 women，through 表示通过这场运动。
lower than for... 比较的是两类女性所对应的死亡率。

sentences.sentence: The children of the newly-literate mothers were also better nourished than those of women who could not read.
sentences.translation: 新近识字母亲的孩子营养状况也优于不会阅读的女性的孩子。

structure.text: The children of the newly-literate mothers
than those of women who could not read

difficult_points: newly-literate 表示刚学会读写的。
those 代替 children，避免重复；who could not read 修饰 women。

sentences.sentence: Why are the children of literate mothers better off?
sentences.translation: 为什么识字母亲的孩子境况更好？
difficult_points: 这是特殊疑问句，why 置于句首。
better off 是固定表达，表示境况更好。

sentences.sentence: According to Peter Sandiford of the Liverpool School of Tropical Medicine, no one knows for certain.
sentences.translation: 利物浦热带医学院的彼得·桑迪福德说，没有人确切知道。

structure.text: According to Peter Sandiford of the Liverpool School of Tropical Medicine

difficult_points: According to 引出信息来源。
for certain 表示确切地，know 后省略了具体内容。

sentences.sentence: Child health was not on the curriculum during the women's lessons, so he and his colleagues are looking at other factors.
sentences.translation: 这些女性上课时，儿童健康并不在课程内容中，因此他和同事正在考察其他因素。

structure.text: so he and his colleagues are looking at other factors

difficult_points: be on the curriculum 表示被列入课程。
so 连接因果，are looking at 表示正在考察。

sentences.sentence: They are working with the same group of 3,000 women, to try to find out whether reading mothers make better use of hospitals and clinics, opt for smaller families, exert more control at home, learn modern child care techniques more quickly, or whether they merely have more respect for themselves and their children.
sentences.translation: 他们正与同一组3000名女性合作，试图查明：识字母亲是否会更好地利用医院和诊所、选择较小家庭、在家中掌握更多控制权、更快学会现代育儿技巧，或是否只是更尊重自己和孩子。

structure.text: are working with the same group of 3,000 women
to try to find out whether reading mothers make better use of hospitals and clinics, opt for smaller families, exert more control at home, learn modern child care techniques more quickly
or whether they merely have more respect for themselves and their children

difficult_points: to try to find out 表示研究目的，whether 引导所要查明的内容。
make、opt、exert、learn 四个动词并列；they 指 reading mothers。

sentences.sentence: The Nicaraguan study may have important implications for governments and aid agencies that need to know where to direct their resources.
sentences.translation: 这项尼加拉瓜研究可能对需要知道将资源投向何处的政府和援助机构具有重要意义。

structure.text: important implications for governments and aid agencies
that need to know where to direct their resources

difficult_points: may have 表示可能具有影响。
that 修饰 governments and aid agencies；where to direct 是疑问词加不定式结构。

sentences.sentence: Sandiford says that there is increasing evidence that female education, at any age, is 'an important health intervention in its own right'.
sentences.translation: 桑迪福德说，越来越多证据表明，任何年龄的女性教育本身就是“一项重要的健康干预”。
difficult_points: 第一个 that 从句作 says 的宾语，第二个 that 说明 evidence 的内容。
at any age 是插入语；in its own right 表示就其本身而言。

sentences.sentence: The results of the study lend support to the World Bank's recommendation that education budgets in developing countries should be increased, not just to help their economies, but also to improve child health.
sentences.translation: 这项研究结果支持世界银行的建议：发展中国家的教育预算应当增加，不仅为了促进经济，也为了改善儿童健康。

structure.text: that education budgets in developing countries should be increased
not just to help their economies, but also to improve child health

difficult_points: that 从句是 recommendation 的同位语，should be increased 是被动结构。
not just...but also... 并列两个增加预算的目的。

sentences.sentence: 'We've known for a long time that maternal education is important,' says John Cleland of the London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine.
sentences.translation: “我们早就知道母亲教育很重要，”伦敦卫生与热带医学院的约翰·克莱兰说。
difficult_points: We've known 是 we have known 的缩写，表示长期以来一直知道。
that 从句说明所知内容，maternal 指母亲的。

sentences.sentence: 'But we thought that even if we started educating girls today, we'd have to wait a generation for the pay-off.
sentences.translation: “但我们原以为，即使今天开始教育女孩，也得等一代人才能看到回报。

structure.text: that even if we started educating girls today, we'd have to wait a generation for the pay-off
even if we started educating girls today

difficult_points: thought 用过去时，表明这是先前的看法；引语在下一句才结束。
even if 引导让步假设，we'd 是 we would 的缩写。

sentences.sentence: The Nicaraguan study suggests we may be able to bypass that.'
sentences.translation: “尼加拉瓜研究表明，我们或许能够绕过这一步。”

structure.text: we may be able to bypass that

difficult_points: suggests 后的 that 被省略；may be able to 表示可能能够。
that 指等待一代人才能见到教育回报这一过程，句尾引号承接前句。

sentences.sentence: Cleland warns that the Nicaraguan crusade was special in many ways, and similar campaigns elsewhere might not work as well.
sentences.translation: 克莱兰警告说，尼加拉瓜的扫盲运动在很多方面都很特殊，其他地方的类似活动未必同样奏效。

structure.text: that the Nicaraguan crusade was special in many ways
and similar campaigns elsewhere might not work as well

difficult_points: that 从句是 warns 的内容，and 后转入其他活动的可能效果。
might not 表示可能不会，as well 指同样好。

sentences.sentence: It is notoriously difficult to teach adults skills that do not have an immediate impact on their everyday lives, and many literacy campaigns in other countries have been much less successful.
sentences.translation: 教成年人学习那些不会立刻影响日常生活的技能，出了名地困难；其他国家许多扫盲活动也远没有那么成功。

structure.text: to teach adults skills that do not have an immediate impact on their everyday lives
that do not have an immediate impact on their everyday lives
and many literacy campaigns in other countries have been much less successful

difficult_points: it 是形式主语，真正主语为 to teach...。
that 修饰 skills；have been 表示到现在为止的结果。

sentences.sentence: 'The crusade was part of a larger effort to bring a better life to the people,' says Cleland.
sentences.translation: “这场运动是让人民过上更好生活的一项更大努力的一部分，”克莱兰说。

structure.text: to bring a better life to the people

difficult_points: to bring... 修饰 effort，说明这项努力的目的。
part of 表示是更大整体中的一部分。

sentences.sentence: Replicating these conditions in other countries will be a major challenge for development workers.
sentences.translation: 在其他国家复制这些条件，将是发展工作者面临的一项重大挑战。

structure.text: Replicating these conditions in other countries
a major challenge for development workers

difficult_points: Replicating... 是动名词短语作主语。
these conditions 指前文尼加拉瓜运动得以成功的特殊条件。
